name: Butter or Sugar Cake on a Baking Sheet
recipeIngredient: 50g butter, margarine, or lard, or 3 tbsp oil
50g almonds (blanched and chopped) or hazelnuts (blanched and chopped)
recipeInstructions:
FOR THE CAKE DOUGH: Cream the yeast with 1 tsp of sugar and 5 tbsp of lukewarm milk. Sieve 2/3 of the flour into a large mixing bowl, making a well in the center and pouring in the yeast mixture. Cover with a 1/4 inch (1/2 cm) layer of flour. Distribute the sugar, flavoring, and melted fat around the well, ensuring they do not come into contact with the yeast. Once the yeast has expanded, gradually stir all ingredients together, starting from the center, and add the remaining milk. Beat the dough with a wooden spoon until it forms bubbles. Knead in the rest of the flour; if the dough is sticky, add a small amount of additional flour, but do not overdo it. Let the dough rise in a warm place until doubled in size. Knead again and roll out on a greased baking sheet. Place a folded piece of greaseproof paper at the open end of the baking sheet.
FOR THE TOPPING: Either spread the fat evenly over the cake or melt it and brush over. Mix the sugar with vanillin sugar and nuts, then sprinkle evenly over the cake. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, then bake. Oven: Preheat for 5 minutes at very hot, bake at moderately hot. Baking Time: about 15 minutes
